title: Pilosella × podolica
taxonomy: Pilosella × podolica is a hybrid species of flowering plant. It is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. The genus Pilosella is part of the tribe Senecioneae, which is a group of plants closely related to the genus Senecio. Pilosella × podolica is a member of the section Pilosella, a group of plants characterized by their yellow or orange flowers. This classification helps to identify and distinguish Pilosella × podolica from other similar species within the Asteraceae family.
uses: Pilosella × podolica has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Eastern Europe, for its purported health benefits. The plant's leaves and stems are said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. They are believed to be effective in treating minor wounds, skin irritations, and other superficial ailments. In traditional remedies, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, or poultices to aid in healing and reduce inflammation. More research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Pilosella × podolica for medicinal purposes.
benefits: Pilosella × podolica is a rare hybrid plant, but research suggests that its close relatives, such as Pilosella officinarum, have been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. However, more research is needed to confirm the specific health benefits of Pilosella × podolica. Traditional uses of these plants include treating digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions. properties: Pilosella × podolica has been studied for its potential therapeutic properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. constituents: Pilosella × podolica contains a range of active constituents, including sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. These compounds contribute to the plant's medicinal properties and are responsible for its biological activities. The sesquiterpene lactones in Pilosella × podolica are particularly notable for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Additionally, the plant's flavonoids and phenolic acids have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antihistamine properties. The combination of these active constituents makes Pilosella × podolica a valuable resource for natural medicine.

intro: Pilosella × podolica, a rare and unique plant species, is a hybrid of Pilosella officinarum and Pilosella caespitosa, belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella × podolica include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pilosella × podolica is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues.
